architecture.summary: The system freezes a pre-trained sparse autoencoder (SAE) inserted after a specific transformer layer, then trains low-rank adapters (LoRA) on the frozen language model's weight matrices in all subsequent MLP and attention sublayers. The SAE reconstructs activations from the original model; these reconstructions propagate through the remaining layers. LoRA adapters (low-rank updates to frozen weights) are trained to minimize KL divergence between the next-token logits of the SAE-modified model and the original model, thus learning to compensate for SAE reconstruction error without modifying the SAE or original model weights.
trained_components: LoRA adapter matrices (U, V for each frozen weight matrix)

biblio.title: Low-Rank Adapting Models for Sparse Autoencoders

claims.dataset: Gemma Scope SAE family (multiple sparsities, widths, layers)
claims.statement: Our method reduces the cross entropy loss gap by 30% to 55% when SAEs are inserted during the forward pass.
Compared to end-to-end (e2e) SAEs, our approach achieves the same downstream cross entropy loss 3× to 20× faster on Gemma and 2× to 10× faster on Llama.
Our approach achieves 130 fewer language model backward passes than e2e SAEs on Gemma-2-2B and 40 fewer backward passes on Llama-3.2-1B.
Training LoRA adapters on just the first layer after the SAE achieves 88.14% of the loss reduction achieved by training LoRA adapters on all layers after the SAE.
For multiple SAEs on Llama-3.1-8B: compound cross entropy loss for 7 SAEs reduced from 7.83 nats to 2.78 nats; for 3 SAEs from 3.55 nats to 2.45 nats.

On SAEBench metrics for Gemma-2-2B TopK SAE: Targeted Probe Perturbation (TPP) max improved from 0.372 to 0.412; Spurious Correlation Removal (SCR) max from 0.448 to 0.526; Sparse Probing (top 1) from 0.732 to 0.760.

claims.dataset: steering evaluation on 500 positive and negative samples (ML, San Francisco, Donald Trump, COVID-19 latents)
claims.statement: The LoRA-adapted model improves at activation steering by scaling SAE latents; for the Donald Trump latent, the LoRA model shows increased likelihood on positive examples while minimizing increases on negative examples.
End-to-end SAEs achieve a lower final cross entropy loss than our method on Llama-3.2-1B, though not on Gemma-2-2B.
